title: cicercula cicera var. amoena
intro: Cicercula cicera var. amoena, also known as the sweet cicely, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Umbelliferae family, characterized by its fern-like leaves and umbrella-like flowers. Traditionally, the plant's root and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, attributed with medicinal properties such as antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of sweet cicely include its ability to aid digestion, reduce flatulence, and alleviate symptoms of colds and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of sweet cicely can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in dried root and leaf forms, often used to add a sweet flavor to digestive remedies.
